content.text:
Based on my analysis, I've identified the root cause of the bug. The `generateId` method in `WritableObjectId.java` always regenerates the ID without checking if an ID already exists. According to the comment, it should reuse an existing ID if one has already been generated, particularly for the "alwaysAsId" case where an ID may be generated but not yet written as a POJO.

Let me look again at the failing test to better understand the scenario. In the test:

1. Two properties reference the same `Bar` object: `bar1` with `alwaysAsId = true` and `bar2` with `alwaysAsId` defaulting to false
2. When `bar1` is processed with `alwaysAsId=true`, its ID gets generated
3. When `bar2` is processed (referencing the same object), it should reuse the same ID instead of generating a new one

The bug is in the `generateId` method - it regenerates the ID even if one already exists.

Identify the root cause of the bug
Apply the fix to WritableObjectId.java
Verify the fix by running the triggering test again
Create the blind_fix.patch file with the diff
